title: OpenAI says Codex data shows work is shifting toward long-running agents
friendlyTitle: OpenAI says people are giving Codex bigger jobs
summary: OpenAI published an economic research note using Codex adoption data to argue that AI work is moving from short chat interactions to delegated, long-horizon tasks. The company says Codex became the primary AI tool across OpenAI departments and that non-developer use has grown especially quickly.
friendlySummary: OpenAI shared data showing that Codex is being used for longer tasks, not just quick answers. It says people outside engineering are also using it more.
whyItMatters: The useful signal is not the vendor framing, but the workflow pattern: more people are asking agents to run multi-step work in parallel instead of asking one question at a time. Teams planning AI adoption should design around reviewable delegation, task queues, and human checkpoints rather than only chatbot prompts.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows how AI may fit into daily work: you give it a task, check progress, and review the result. It is less like a search box and more like a junior teammate that needs supervision.
